TextENGNew: I'd eventually made some friends at my Okinomiya school, even though I'd transferred in mid-year.
Since relatives would occasionally let me work part-time at their shops, I never wanted for pocket money.
I was currently living at my parents' home in Okinomiya, so I had no living expenses to worry about.
Having my entire paycheck become spending money was wonderfully liberating.
The room Kasai had arranged for me back then still served as my hideout from time to time.
It was incredibly convenient when I didn't want to see my parents' faces.
As it happened, today was one of those days when I'd planned to hole up there.
``...Damn that teacher!@k@vS06/06/150601317. She went and told my parents I'd been cutting class!``
My dad had blown his top, making home uninhabitable. That was my own fault, I suppose.
You'd think the teacher would know that my father's a yakuza and gets apocalyptically terrifying when angry. A little discretion would be nice.
That life wasn't terrible, honestly. Normal to a fault, but not bad.
changeReason: Original adds the concept of 'boring' not present in the Japanese; new translation more accurately reflects ノーマル (normal) with 'Normal to a fault, but not bad.'

TextENGNew: The reason I could appreciate such an ordinary existence was surely because of the experience of living in hiding.
The sheer relief of being able to use your own name openly -- nothing could replace that.
...Well, I could write it off as a valuable experience.@kEnough time had passed for that.
changeReason: Original adds 'I think I learned from it' not present in the Japanese; new translation correctly renders それだけの時間が経過していた as 'Enough time had passed for that.'

TextENGNew: I was shopping not just for tonight's dinner, but stocking up for a week-long retreat at the hideout.
changeReason: New translation includes the detail from Japanese that she was shopping 'not just for tonight's dinner' (今夜のおかずだけでなく), which the original omitted.

TextENGNew: My filially pious reasoning: if they couldn't see my face for a while, my parents would learn to appreciate me more.
``Huh? Oh, wait!@k@vS06/06/150601319. Isn't there a hundred-yen sale today?@k@vS06/06/150601320. Yes, that's right! Definitely!``
I had enough financial sense to pinch my pocket money when I could.
In high spirits, I swung both arms wide and spun on my heel.
That little whirlwind smacked its arm right into a motorcycle parked rudely across the sidewalk.
changeReason: New translation preserves the つむじ風 (whirlwind) metaphor from the Japanese, referring to Shion's spinning motion as a whirlwind; the original dropped this metaphor.

TextENGNew: ``...Ow!!@k@vS06/06/150601322. Wh-... Geez!!@k@vS06/06/150601323. Who parked their bikes here?!``
Still riding high, I kicked the offending motorcycle without a second thought.
There were three motorcycles in a row.
They toppled like dominoes.
It was such a beautiful chain reaction that even I had to admire it.
...Wait, huh...?
A strange sense of deja vu.@k...Had this happened before...?
While I was blinking, trying to recall the memory, someone grabbed me by the collar and dragged me into a dim alley.
changeReason: Original says 'grabbed me by the neck'; new translation correctly renders 襟首 (collar) as 'grabbed me by the collar.'

TextENGNew: Three rough-looking men who appeared to own the bikes started screaming at me.
...There it was again, that deeply uncanny deja vu.
``Dafuq'd ya jus' do?! I'mma freakin'... graahhh!!``
``Yer dead, ya hear me?! Yer freakin' DEAD!!``
``Grraaah!!@k@vS06/00/huryoC15004. Think ya can jus' mess with our stuff?!``
That's right.@k...It was when I first met Satoshi-kun.
That time... Satoshi-kun had leapt out to save me.
changeReason: Original omits the temporal context あの時 (at that time) and the vivid action 飛び出して (leapt out) present in the Japanese; new translation includes both.

TextENGNew: ...If I pretended to cry again now...
...Would Satoshi-kun come to my rescue again...?
I made a little wish, like a charm.
changeReason: New translation includes the おまじないの感覚 (like a charm/incantation) nuance present in Japanese; original reduced it to just 'I made a wish.'

TextENGNew: ...And without shame, I began faking tears and pleading for mercy.
Just then.
I... couldn't believe my ears.
``...Cut it out.@k@vS06/01/150100002. Can't you see she's crying already...?!!``
It was a boy around my age.
The backlight from the bright street made his face hard to see.
Of course... it couldn't be him.
...Such a convenient dream couldn't possibly come true...
changeReason: Original heavily abbreviates the Japanese; new translation faithfully renders そんな都合のいい夢が、あるわけがない (such a convenient dream couldn't possibly come true).

TextENGNew: And when I shook myself awake, the silhouette that had resembled Satoshi-kun was... someone else entirely.
Compared to Satoshi-kun... this one had a rougher, less refined air.
If Satoshi-kun was someone raised in pristine purity, this boy was covered in scrapes and scars -- tough as nails.
changeReason: New translation adds 'tough as nails' which captures 打たれ強そう (looks resilient/tough), a nuance the original omitted.

TextENGNew: Completely different, and yet... somehow, I sensed something of Satoshi-kun in him.
``What da hell...?!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15015. Tryin' to play hero?!!``
``Think yer tough?! Wanna find out?!``
``Say somethin'!!@k@vS06/00/huryoC15006. We'll beat it outta ya!!``
Berated by all three, the boy did look... somewhat rattled.
``Oh, well, um... she's already crying, so maybe you could, you know, let her off...? Ahaha...``
``HuuUUh?!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15017. Who asked you?!``
``Whoa whoa whoa!!@k@vS06/01/150100005. Time out!@k@vS06/01/150100006. Violence is bad, you know...!!@k@vS06/01/150100007. Ahaha...!``
...Who was this guy...?@kI felt embarrassed for having mistaken him for Satoshi-kun, even for an instant.
His entrance had been mildly cool, I'd give him that... but compared to Satoshi-kun, he was a total letdown.
``Well, I mean... it's really unfortunate about your bikes...@k@vS06/01/150100009. But perhaps you could find it in your hearts to...@k@vS06/01/150100010. What would it take to smooth this over...?``
``Of course, neither of us has any money on us, you know?@k@vS06/01/150100012. Ahaha...``
``Hmph... if ya ain't got the cash, then...``
``No money means no money!@k@vS06/00/huryoB15006. Guess ya gotta pay with yer body!!``
``Y'know, this chick's actually pretty hot up close...!@k@vS06/00/huryoC15008. How 'bout after this... heh heh heh heh!!``
...Seriously?@kYou've got to be joking.
I tightened my grip on the stun gun I kept hidden for self-defense.
Maximum output. Don't blame me if it kills you, you absolute morons...
changeReason: Original translates 大バカタレども as 'pervs'; new translation more accurately renders it as 'absolute morons,' matching the Japanese insult meaning 'utter fools.'

TextENGNew: ``I always wanted a cute girl on my arm to show off around town!!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15020. Huff, huff!``
``Yeah yeah, get all dolled up, put on a miniskirt... ohhhh man, I'm getting excited!!``
...These guys were getting drunk on their own fantasies. Actually working themselves up.
...Hey, why are they turning toward me?
changeReason: Original adds 'perverts' and changes 'turning toward me' to 'closing in on me' which aren't in the Japanese 振り返る (turn toward); new translation is more faithful.

TextENGNew: Their groping, grasping hands were truly revolting...@k...Was I in danger here? In more ways than one?
``Mhaaah, tight minis are awesoooome!!``
``Mini mini, ohhh yeahhh!!@k@vS06/00/huryoB15009. Haahh, so goood!!!``
``Make her sexy from head to toe!@k@vS06/00/huryoC15010. Maximum skin! We'll dress her up in the ultimate outfit!!!``
``Hey, you can't be serious?!@k@vS06/06/150601326. Keep your filthy hands off me!!!``
Just then, a flash of light cut through the air...@kThat boy -- what was he--?!
``You absolute MORONS!!!!``
changeReason: Original uses 'stupid fuuucks' for ボケナスども; new translation uses 'absolute MORONS,' which better matches the Japanese insult without the excessive vulgarity.

TextENGNew: Lightning-fast blows struck, and the three punks went down one after another!!
Whoa, this kid was actually strong?!
``What da hell?!!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15023. Ya want some?!!!``
``Guhhh?!``
changeReason: Original translates the pain grunt ぐほあッ as 'Huuuhh?!' (confusion); new translation renders it as 'Guhhh?!' which better captures the sound of being struck.

TextENGNew: ``You don't understand!!@k@vS06/01/150100015. You just don't get it!!!!``
``Wh-What dafuq?!!!``
``There's zero moe appeal in a tight mini!!!@k@vS06/01/150100017. And exposed skin should be kept to a minimum!!@k@vS06/01/150100018. Even if the sun rises in the west!!``
changeReason: Original translates 萌えがない as 'nothing arousing,' conflating moe with sexual arousal; new translation correctly says 'zero moe appeal,' preserving the distinction central to the character's lecture.

TextENGNew: ``That is the absolute, ironclad law of the moe world!!!``
changeReason: Original omits 萌え業界 (the moe industry/world) from 'ironclad rule'; new translation includes it, preserving the character's framing of moe as an entire industry with its own laws.

TextENGNew: ``Listen up, you amateurs.@k@vS06/01/150100021. What separates Homo sapiens from animals?``
``That's right -- clothing!@k@vS06/01/150100023. A human is only truly human because of the fabrics adorning their skin!!!``
changeReason: Original paraphrases loosely as 'without clothes are just animals'; new translation more accurately renders the Japanese concept that humans are defined by the adornment of clothing beyond bare skin.

TextENGNew: ``Which means you lot, craving nothing but bare skin, are sub-human!!@k@vS06/01/150100025. You're no better than animals!!@k@vS06/01/150100026. I will re-educate every last one of you!!@k@vS06/01/150100027. Fall in line, maggots!!``
``Pffgh!!``
``Ghaaa!!!``
``You brought up tight miniskirts, didn't you?``
``Suppose there's a girl standing right here, worthy of the title 'beautiful.'@k@vS06/01/150100030. But 'beautiful girl' is far too broad a category.``
``Lecturing you on that would be harder than explaining samurai aesthetics to a foreigner who knows nothing of Japan beyond sushi, mountains, and geisha!!!``
``So I'll limit the lesson to the most widely recognized school uniforms!!@k@vS06/01/150100033. Name the Big Three! Answer me!!``
``Correct: regular uniforms, gym uniforms, and school swimsuits.@k@vS06/01/150100035. Sailor versus blazer preferences fall under 'regular uniform.'``
``Same goes for bloomers versus shorts!!@k@vS06/01/150100037. Navy versus white swimsuits -- different colors, same category!!!@k@vS06/01/150100038. Getting the picture?!``
``Now suppose each of you three favors one category!!``
``You -- Delinquent A! Regular uniforms!@k@vS06/01/150100041. Delinquent B! Gym uniforms! And Delinquent C -- school swimsuits!!!``
``Picture it in your mind! Three seconds!!!@k@vS06/01/150100043. Done?! If you can't even fantasize right, you lack spirit! Do it over!!!``
``Now imagine the girl of your dreams standing here in that very outfit! Believe it! Commit! Put your soul into it!!@k@vS06/01/150100045. Only acceptable answers: 'Sir yes sir!'``
``You call yourselves soldiers?!!!!``
``Good, looks like you've all got the image. Moving on.``
``Now compare your moe outfit with that sleazy tight miniskirt you perverts were drooling over!``
``See?!! Completely incompatible!!!@k@vS06/01/150100050. Sexiness and moe point in entirely different directions -- they're oil and water!!``
``The number of philistines who can't tell moe from sex appeal has been growing, and it's truly deplorable!!``
``Sex appeal is instinct! But moe is wabi-sabi -- it belongs to the world of mono no aware!!``
changeReason: New translation preserves the specific Japanese aesthetic terms わび寂 (wabi-sabi) and もののあはれ (mono no aware) that the original paraphrased as 'delicate and rustic sensibilities.'

TextENGNew: ``If instinct is your only lens for appreciating a woman's charm, you're dogs, monkeys, and pheasants!!``
``Take your kibidango and get lost on Onigashima!!!``
changeReason: Original drops the Momotaro fairy tale references (キビダンゴ/kibidango and 鬼ヶ島/Onigashima); new translation preserves them, maintaining the character's comedic cultural allusion.

TextENGNew: ``Get back here!!``
``Incidentally, with the recent cultural thaw between East and West, all sorts of new moe varieties have been landing on our shores.``
``You didn't know that?! Ignoramus!!``
changeReason: Original translates 愚か者 as 'Get out of here' (a command); new translation renders it as 'Ignoramus' (an insult), which is what the Japanese actually says.

TextENGNew: ``The school uniform is Japanese culture! It's art!!!``
``There is no room for East-meets-West fusion in this world!!!@k@vS06/01/150100061. Are you even listening, you spineless squid?!!!``
``Grit your teeth -- today's training is merciless!!!@k@vS06/01/150100063. You're not sleeping until you understand true moe culture!!!``
``Here it comes! Guidance! Guidance! GUIDANCE!!!``
changeReason: Original translates 指導指導指導 (guidance/training x3) as 'Here comes my whip'; new translation preserves the repeated word 'Guidance! Guidance! GUIDANCE!' matching the Japanese.

TextENGNew: Just then, the sound of several people running reached my ears.
``Maebara-san!!@k@vS06/24/152400002. I gathered some people!!!``
I saw several kids running toward us.@k Seemed like our backup had arrived.
``Aw shit!@k@vS06/00/huryoA15027. Les' get oudda here...!``
The three punks had just been standing there agape, but they started running away when they saw that many people...
``You're awesome, Maebara-san...!@k@vS06/25/152500002. You had those punks transfixed, all by yourself...!``
``One of them even had a nosebleed...@k@vS06/24/152400004. You're so strong, Maebara-san!``
``...Whew...@k@vS06/01/150100066. It seems my personal aura has grown more powerful.
If this keeps up, I'll soon surpass even Coach's 'Maid in Heaven' Reality Marble! ...Anyway,@k@vS06/01/150100068. are you all right, Mion?``
The boy was apparently called Maebara. He turned around to face me.
...Even the way he mistook me for Mion sounded like Satoshi-kun.
...But Satoshi-kun isn't a pervert like him!
``I was anxious to see how you'd fight back, but...
I guess three guys were too many, huh?@k@vS06/01/150100071. I don't believe it! Aha ha ha ha ha!``
I was still confused... I couldn't yet grasp the situation.
...Who was this guy?!@k Mion has friends like him?!
I was still spacing out in shock.
So... I forgot to move@k until his hand touched my head...
``Well, you know...@k@vS06/01/150100073. I'll forget that I saw you cry,@k@vS06/01/150100074. so... cheer up, okay?``
Rub, rub.
...The boy called Maebara stroked my head roughly.
It didn't feel good.@k It hurt, actually.
But still... his hand had the same warmth.
Later on, Mion told me about him.
He was called Keiichi Maebara.
Kei-chan for short.@k He's one grade below us.
Not only that, but he just moved to Hinamizawa...
Thanks to his attitude and lack of manners, he'd already become an idol in their class.
Mion joyously explained all of this.
...Sure, he sounded like a fun kid.
I knew why Mion was into him.
...But...@k Satoshi-kun is way cooler.
He wasn't worth stealing from Sis...... Or was he?
My heart throbbed.@k I was excited.
It felt like I'd gotten a new toy.
I was curious as to what kind of relationship Mion intended to build with him...@k I'd love to tease her about it.
``Thanks, Kei-chan.@k@vS06/06/150601326_2. Let me buy you a drink.@k@vS06/06/150601326_3. My uncle's restaurant is just nearby, so why don't we go there?``
Satoshi-kun would refuse out of modesty.
But Keiichi Maebara said 'okay' within two seconds.
Well then...@k I couldn't wait to tease the two of them...!
My boring life suddenly became bright again.
``Whoa... H-Hey, don't cling to my arm...``
``Oh, don't you like it?``
``Uh, well... I don't hate it, but... it's just your breasts that are touching me, and...``
``My breasts bother you?@k@vS06/06/150601329. Hee hee hee hee!``
Keiichi Maebara was fun.
Mion thought the same way I did.
She would always talk about him.
I was happy for her. It made me smile.
``I guess Kei-chan is really your favorite,@k@vS06/06/150601331. huh, Sis?@k@vS06/06/150601332. What's the most interesting thing about him?``
``Ahahahaha... Let's see...
To put it plainly, he's just funny.@k@vS06/03/150300278. For example, just a couple of days ago in the club...!``
Mion really liked him, but didn't seem to think it was love yet.
She's reserved in that area, so that was really like her.
As for me, well,@k I didn't hate him.
But my feelings weren't the same as Mion's.
Kei-chan was just too childish compared to Satoshi-kun.
.........I wondered how Satoshi-kun was doing...
Was he waiting for things to cool down somewhere far away...?
The case had already been solved, though. That dopehead was the murderer.
There was no reason for him to wait anymore...
I shook my head...@k I shouldn't think about it too much.
Oh, wait. I know.
The case was confidential, so the arrest of the culprit hadn't been made public.
So, Satoshi-kun hadn't realized that he could come back.
...I know he's a little thickheaded.
Maybe he'd be waiting for things to calm down until the end of time.
...But if so... couldn't he give a call to Satoko at least?
He should tell her he's living in a faraway place,@k but that he'd be coming home soon...
...Ahh... That stupid Satoko.
She'd been living with Rika Furude since Satoshi-kun went away.
So nobody would pick up the phone even if he called...@k Stupid girl!@k Don't you want your big brother to come home?
Given Satoshi-kun's personality...
He wouldn't remember any phone numbers other than his home number...@k So, he wouldn't be able to contact anybody in Hinamizawa...
Maybe he's been calling Satoko every night. He keeps ringing, but nobody picks up...
Then he'd assume Satoko might be taking a bath, or be in the restroom......@k He'd put on a puzzled smile while mumbling ``mmph...`` like always...
I imitated his voice without realizing it.
``...Didn't you say something, Shion?``
I laughed it off and urged Mion to continue.
I realized then that her tone had changed.
I hadn't been listening to her because I was thinking about Satoshi-kun.
I'd thought she was still happily talking about Kei-chan.
......But Mion sounded grim now.
Her faint smile looked pitiful.
It was too late to ask what was going on.@k So instead, I concentrated on her story instead.
...Apparently, Keiichi had tactfully offered her a doll as a gift, but she turned it down.

Good grief, girl... Men aren't that hard. If you just sincerely accepted it and responded with a wonderful smile, his heart would have been sent aflutter,@k causing great tides in your relationship to come.
What a shame, indeed.
Her inability to read the room and how she gets cold feet times like these is why she has such bad luck with boys.
It's a bit weird saying this as her twin, but she is attractive to the point that nobody would complain if you called her beautiful.
And yet from what I can hear, Mion's classmate Rena Ryugu is more charming and is more likely to cause perceivable feelings of love in her classmates.
So should it happen that these two end up competing with eachother for Keiichi... Mion would have little chance of winning. The chance may as well be zero.
``...After all that,@k@vS06/03/150300283. Uncle gave everybody a doll as a thank-you gift.@k@vS06/03/150300284. Everybody except for me.``
``Okay,@k@vS06/06/150601337. except for you.``
``Uh... I wanted one, too,@k@vS06/03/150300286. but... I couldn't say it in front of everybody......``
``You still can't express your needs, huh...?@k@vS06/06/150601339. And then?``
Mion sniffled.
...I realized that she was on the verge of tears.
``......The cutest doll was in Kei-chan's bag.``
``And you wanted that doll?``
``...It didn't matter which one!@k@vS06/03/150300289. ...But although Kei-chan knew only I didn't get one, he didn't give it to me...@k@vS06/03/150300290. hic... he didn't give it to me...
He said the doll wouldn't fit me and...@k@vS06/03/150300292. that I should be born as a boy in my next life...@k@vS06/03/150300293. That's what he said... hic...``
...That was Keiichi's weak point.
He might be fun and popular.
...But he wasn't mature......@k not like Satoshi-kun.
``...I want to start all over...@k@vS06/03/150300295. I want to go back to when I just met him and start all over... I want him to treat me like a girl from the beginning...``

``Don't take it too seriously.@k@vS06/06/150601343. It'll be okay, because it's just you who wants to start over while he didn't even notice anything wrong.@k@vS06/06/150601344. It'd be harder if you guys fought over it or something.``
``...Can I start over...?``
``If you didn't cry in front of him, it's not too late.@k@vS06/06/150601346. You can cry as much as you want now, and if you show him a different side of you from tomorrow on, you can still fix it.``

``I'm amazed how insensitive he is, but part of that is just the impression you've been giving him so far.@k@vS06/06/150601348. If you change your attitude from tomorrow on, you can still change his mind.``
``How do I change my attitude...? How...?``

``...If I tell you to act cute, you'd start going to school in frilly clothes...@k@vS06/06/150601350. So don't think, and just be natural.@k@vS06/06/150601351. But, without overdoing it, pay attention to how you behave around him.
Ah, and don't forget to be cautious of Rena!@k@vS06/06/150601353. She's a bit ahead of you so far.@k@vS06/06/150601354. Kei-chan might prefer Rena at this point.``
``......Ooh... it's true... Rena's really girly...@k@vS06/03/150300300. She's cute, and caring...``

``That just means you can't slack off for even a moment, Sis, because you've got a rival.@k@vS06/06/150601356. Tears can attract men, but they don't anchor them.@k@vS06/06/150601357. You should always be smiling, but cry when it really counts!
Hmm, that's how you typically do it...@k@vS06/06/150601359. Maybe I should try that on Kei-chan myself......``
``Eh, n-no, don't...!``
``Ahahahahahaha...!@k@vS06/06/150601361. He's the boy you like,@k@vS06/06/150601362. so I guess it's natural that I like him too.
Hmm, how can I attract him...?@k@vS06/06/150601364. Hey, Sis, why don't we have a competition over him?``
``No, no!@k@vS06/03/150300303. Never!!@k@vS06/03/150300304. I'll kick you into orbit if you come near Hinamizawa!!``
``Aha ha ha ha, aha ha ha ha!``
I'm happy for you, Sis.@k It's not too late at all.
It's important to know the distance between the boy you love and yourself, and shift the way you appeal to him to compensate.
......The boy I love is...... missing, you know?
I don't even know where he is,@k or when he's coming back.
Compared to that, you're very lucky.
Mion was saying she'd do her best from tomorrow on with red tearful eyes... but I sensed a deeply negative feeling emerging inside me.
Nobody was blaming Mion for being happy with Keiichi.
I wasn't envious.
I wasn't jealous.
...I didn't want her to be blamed the same way I was blamed.
...It was just that there was no space for Satoshi-kun in Mion's mind anymore,@k filled as it was with Kei-chan and her other friends now.
It didn't matter to her if Satoshi-kun came back@k or not,@k just like how she didn't care if it was sunny or rainy tomorrow.
What about me, then?
Had I been thinking about Satoshi-kun all the time for the past year...?
...I wasn't proud of my own answer.
...I'd become used to life without Satoshi-kun...
His absence didn't hurt me anymore...
...I was getting used to life with Keiichi Maebara...
And if I let my guard down... he might just fill up the space I'd been saving for Satoshi-kun...
If Shion Sonozaki had prayed harder for Satoshi-kun to return, he might've come home already......
......Where did Satoshi-kun go...?

Last year, right after his disappearance, I'd paid close attention to the information circulating in the Sonozaki family and its underground connections,@k and I frequently went to the police station to check with Ooishi for new information.
Even with all that, I couldn't get any new leads on his whereabouts.
......I eventually became interested in Oyashiro-sama's curse, which surrounded Satoshi-kun's disappearance...@k I researched the dark history of the village...
......The more I looked into it, the less likely his survival seemed.
...I'd returned Miyo-san's scrapbooks to her.
...And then, I hadn't gone to the library since.
I started a new life in a new school.
...I'd made new friends.
.........And in the same way...@k as falling leaves cover the ground...@k I felt like I was losing sight of what was really important to me...
It was already almost the end of spring.
They said that summer was coming early, and it'd be very hot this year.
Normally it'd be the rainy season now, but we'd had no rain at all.
The Watanagashi festival was right around the corner.
It'd been almost a year... since Satoshi-kun went missing.
In Hinamizawa, they don't allow you to talk openly about Oyashiro-sama's curse, or the series of mysterious deaths.
......So, everybody acts like the murder of Satoshi-kun's aunt never happened.
That's because Satoshi Houjou-kun was a member of the family of traitors.
...He fell victim to Oyashiro-sama's curse on the fourth year running. He was demoned away.
...Everybody was trying to forget about his existence, and erase their memories of him...
Don't make him disappear because of the curse.
Don't erase his existence.
Satoshi-kun was living in Hinamizawa just a year ago.
He went to school.
He was an A student.
He was a member of the Hinamizawa Fighters. He could never hit the ball when it really mattered.
He was unreliable, but pure.@k He was warm!
Because of the curse, people made him disappear. ......They stopped mentioning his name and acted like he never existed.
.........A new resident like Keiichi Maebara wouldn't even know him...
...He seriously believed that Satoshi-kun had transferred out...
I miss you, Satoshi-kun......
I want to go shopping with you again.
......I want to be in a baseball game with you again... and get a cold drink afterwards.
......I want to tease you, and... I want you to stroke my head again.
......Your hand was so warm......@k I felt as if my whole body was being caressed.
